findings.issue: Typo `cleint` on line 298 — undefined variable passed to obj$OpenAI(). Function crashes immediately on call.
findings.rationale: Function cannot execute. Any caller hits 'cleint not defined'. Breaking defect for OpenAI representation.

findings.issue: Sys.getenv('OPENAI_API_KEY') returns empty string (not NULL) if unset. `length(open_ai_key) == 0` never matches empty string. Silent auth failure instead of clear error.
findings.rationale: User won't know API key is missing until OpenAI rejects the request. Weak error handling masks real problem.
findings.proposed_fix: Add nzchar() validation and rlang::abort() if credential missing
findings.new_code_snippet: if (length(open_ai_key) == 0 || !nzchar(open_ai_key)) { open_ai_key <- Sys.getenv('OPENAI_API_KEY'); if (!nzchar(open_ai_key)) rlang::abort('OPENAI_API_KEY not set') }

findings.issue: HARDCODED LIVE OPENAI API KEY on line 518: sk-YJ4kEnx... Exposed in git history across multiple commits (0b3792e, 8188e5c). Anonymous function never called, never exported — dead code containing a live credential.
findings.rationale: ABSOLUTE SECURITY FAILURE. Key is in version control and any mirror/archive. Key is now compromised. Function is unreachable dead code.
findings.proposed_fix: DELETE lines 503-525 entirely. User must revoke the key at platform.openai.com.
findings.new_code_snippet: # DELETED: dead code with exposed credential

findings.issue: distinct_matches dedup is a no-op. `mutate(row = 1:n())` inside group_by resets row numbers to 1 per group, so `filter(row == min(row))` matches every row. Deduplication collapses to identity.
findings.rationale: distinct_matches feature silently returns unfiltered data. Users think they're getting unique pairs but get everything.
findings.proposed_fix: Use slice_head(n=1) instead of grouped mutate-filter

findings.issue: tidyr::unnest() without explicit cols= argument. tidyr >= 1.0 requires explicit cols or crashes.
findings.rationale: polyfuzz_match will error on any system with tidyr >= 1.0 (i.e., all modern R installs since 2020).

findings.issue: Off-by-one in topic numbering. `1:ncol(data) - 1` produces (0, 1, ..., ncol-2), missing last topic. Should cover 0..(ncol-1).
findings.rationale: Approximate distribution output misaligned with bert_topic_info() join keys. Silent data corruption.

findings.issue: bert_topic_info() result passed to left_join() with no nrow check. If model not fitted, silent join on empty tibble.
findings.rationale: Defensive programming failure. Users get empty tibble with no error.
findings.proposed_fix: Add nrow check and abort with clear message
findings.new_code_snippet: tbl_count <- bert_topic_info(...); if (nrow(tbl_count) == 0) rlang::abort('Model not fitted'); data <- data |> left_join(tbl_count, ...)

findings.issue: obj$umap_model$embedding_ accessed with no fit check. Returns empty tibble if unfitted.
findings.rationale: Silent empty result when UMAP model not trained.
findings.proposed_fix: Check obj$umap_model exists before accessing embedding_
findings.new_code_snippet: if (length(obj$umap_model) == 0) rlang::abort('UMAP model not fitted'); embedding_ <- obj$umap_model$embedding_

patterns_across_files: DEAD STATEMENT: `! 'mod' %>% exists() & assign_to_environment` appears across 20+ import functions — computed but discarded
WEAK CREDENTIAL HANDLING: length() check but not nzchar() for env vars, so empty strings pass validation
DEPRECATED PURRR: flatten_df/flatten_chr/flatten_dbl used across bertopic.R, utils-internal.R, other_utils.R (batch C doesn't hit them but pattern exists codebase-wide)
steelman: Package shows strong design intent (reticulate Python wrapping, tibble output contract, parameterized rep models). Critical bugs are in low-frequency code paths (OpenAI repr, distinct_matches flag, approx_distribution munge) so basic smoke tests pass. Architectural composition is sound.
